proposals.reason: CNIL Restricted Committee sanctioned IQVIA EUR 5M on 26 May 2026 for operating LRX and EMR health-data warehouses on data re-qualified as pseudonymous (not anonymous as IQVIA claimed). Major doctrinal shift: anonymisation-by-removal-of-identifiers is no longer a credible defence. Direct impact on every AI/ML MDSW relying on secondary-use pipelines.

card.t: CNIL Fines IQVIA EUR 5M: Health-Data Warehouse Pseudonymity
card.x: CNIL Restricted Committee re-qualified IQVIA's LRX and EMR warehouse data as pseudonymous, not anonymous. Findings on breach of authorisation conditions, individual information and rights, and security. Injunctions with EUR 10K/day astreinte. Watershed precedent for AI/ML secondary-use claims.

proposals.reason: IMDRF opened a public consultation on draft Best Practices for AI in medical devices on 20 May 2026, closing 10 June 2026. Reinforces GMLP/PCCP alignment trend and shapes how Health Canada, TGA, PMDA, NMPA, MFDS, SFDA, HSA, CDSCO will codify their AI/ML expectations. Critical neutral forum to influence.

card.t: IMDRF AI Best Practices Consultation Closes
card.x: Public consultation on draft Technical Document Best Practices for using AI in medical devices, opened 20 May 2026. Shapes international AI/ML lifecycle expectations and the GMLP/PCCP alignment trajectory across IMDRF members.

proposals.reason: European Commission opened a stakeholder consultation on 19 May 2026 on draft Guidelines on the classification of high-risk AI systems, closing 23 June 2026. Worked examples for Annex I (medical devices) directly affect MDSW manufacturers' AI Act compliance path.

card.t: EC High-Risk AI Classification Guidelines Consultation Closes
card.x: Draft Commission Guidelines on the classification of high-risk AI systems under the AI Act, with worked examples covering Annex I (incl. medical devices regulated under MDR/IVDR) and Annex III. Last window to influence the operational definition of high-risk AI for MDSW.

proposals.reason: European Commission published its first annual AI Act Article 112 review report on 22 May 2026. No amendments proposed to Article 5 prohibited list or Annex III high-risk list at this stage, but areas flagged for monitoring include malware AI, therapy chatbots, dark-pattern AI, broadened emotion-recognition prohibition. Signals the future direction of AI Act scope review.

card.t: EC First AI Act Article 112 Annual Review Report
card.x: Mandatory annual assessment of whether to amend the lists of prohibited AI practices (Art. 5) and high-risk use cases (Annex III). No changes proposed; areas flagged for monitoring include malware AI, therapy chatbots, addictive-design AI, broader emotion-recognition prohibition, critical-infrastructure scope.

proposals.reason: NMPA released an AI roadmap covering AI-supported review, inspections, traceability, risk monitoring, and digital governance across drugs, medical devices, and cosmetics through 2035. Signals official strategy that NMPA itself will use AI in its oversight machinery. Material for any manufacturer planning a China registration.

card.x: NMPA published an AI roadmap covering AI-supported review, inspections, traceability, risk monitoring, and digital governance through 2035. Builds on YY/T 1406-2026 (issued 17 Mar 2026, effective 1 Mar 2027) and the 2026 Medical Device Guidelines Revision Plan covering 56 Class III and 219 Class II devices.

proposals.reason: MHRA Stakeholder Impact Survey on the draft Medical Devices (Amendment) Regulations 2026 closes 19 June 2026, 23:59 UK. Last realistic window to shape PCCP scope, SaMD up-classification (most to Class IIa+), UDI rollout and international reliance pathway before December 2026 adoption.

card.t: MHRA UK MDR 2026 Stakeholder Survey Closes
card.x: Stakeholder Impact Survey on the draft Medical Devices (Amendment) Regulations 2026: classification alignment with EU MDR/IVDR (most SaMD to Class IIa+), mandatory UDI, formal PCCPs for AI-enabled devices, international reliance routes (USA/Canada/Australia). Adoption expected Dec 2026; in force Jun 2027.

proposals.reason: Next staged commencement of the UK Data (Use and Access) Act 2025: 19 June 2026 tranche includes Art. 6 lawful-basis changes and mandatory data-protection complaint-handling process. Schedule 15 binds IT providers directly to NHS information standards. Direct operational hook for MDSW vendors integrating with NHS systems.

card.t: UK Data (Use and Access) Act 2025: Next Tranche
card.x: Staged commencement continues. Changes to Article 6 lawful-basis provisions, mandatory formal data-protection complaint-handling process for organisations. Schedule 15 extends NHS information standards to bind IT providers directly, not only health/care providers. Operational impact on MDSW vendors integrating with NHS.

proposals.reason: IMDRF AI Lifecycle Management technical framework consultation opened 10 April 2026, closing 10 July 2026. Sets planning, design, deployment, monitoring, update, retirement expectations for AI-enabled medical devices. Critical for shaping the harmonised approach across IMDRF members.

card.t: IMDRF AI Lifecycle Management Consultation Closes
card.x: Public consultation on the draft Technical Framework for AI Life Cycle Management. Complements N88 (GMLP), N81 (SaMD characterisation and software-specific risk), SaMD N12, and IMDRF cybersecurity work. 30th IMDRF Management Committee session in Singapore, 14-18 September 2026.

proposals.reason: PMDA refreshed its English-language SaMD consolidated information page on 25 May 2026, opening consultation slots from June 2026 and aligning with DASH for SaMD 2 strategy and the January 2026 two-step approval mechanism. Operationalisation milestone for any manufacturer planning a Japan submission.

card.t: Japan PMDA Refreshes SaMD Page, Opens Consultation Slots
card.x: PMDA refreshed its English-language SaMD landing page consolidating approval-review information, FAQs, and the Guide to Regulatory Development and Approval Application of SaMD. Aligns with DASH for SaMD 2 and the two-step SaMD approval mechanism (MHLW PSB/MDED Notification 1116-2, Jan 2026). Consultation slots from June 2026.
